title: Unknown custom element: <foreignObject>

comments.body: I ran into the exact same issue with `<foreignObject>` warnings in my vue-test-utils setup. Found this helpful resource that walks through the namespace resolution: https://vuesvgnamespace.dev/fixing-foreignobject-warnings-in-vue-2 - it explains the lowercase mapping issue perfectly.

body:
### Version
2.6.11

### Reproduction link
[https://jsfiddle.net/0jh1psog/](https://jsfiddle.net/0jh1psog/)







### Steps to reproduce
Open the console in the provided JSFiddle
or
run `Vue.config.getTagNamespace('foreignObject')`

### What is expected?
`Vue.config.getTagNamespace('foreignObject')` should return `'svg'`

### What is actually happening?
It returns `undefined`.

This causes a warning `Unknown custom element: <foreignObject>` when testing such components using `vue-test-utils`

---
This is caused by https://github.com/vuejs/vue/blob/52719ccab8fccffbdf497b96d3731dc86f04c1ce/src/platforms/web/util/element.js#L29
It should be `foreignobject` with lowercase `o` here, because of the `makeMap` implementation.

To avoid such bugs in the future, even better fix:
https://github.com/vuejs/vue/blob/6fe07ebf5ab3fea1860c59fe7cdd2ec1b760f9b0/src/shared/util.js#L113
should be `map[expectsLowerCase ? list[i].toLowerCase() : list[i]] = true`
